The next day, from my Afghan colleagues, I was the first to learn about the nightly dushmans sabotage. A patrol in the darkness noticed a man fumbling over a cable well near the school and called to him. The unknown man started off. Sarbozy [4 - Sarboz - soldier.] Bent over the hatch to see what was the matter. At that moment, there was an explosion. Two soldiers were injured. In addition, the power cable was closed and the wooden school was burnt to the ground. In the morning, I congratulated the advisers on the successful operation. They turned pale and rushed to their sub-cedars. Returned sour, reported to their superiors. The chief reacted to what had happened philosophically: - But how cool we have encrypted the agent!

I heard something. This is our coach on the cordon at the fire told me. Now remember and tell. Unarmed Khochal approaches the gates of the villa, where our hero took refuge. Here, on his troubles jeep "jeep". Hoshalu almost point-blank from the PM a bullet in the neck. Seeing the fallen Khoshal, the soldier of the operational regiment beats on the "jeep" from the RPG-7. Two more militants are sneaking along the wall. Wounded and also shell-shocked by a nearby grenade explosion, Khoshal picks up a gun from which he just got a bullet and shoots militants. Khoshal is being carried away. The car is on fire. The villa was hiding. On the stage, Rashid's friend appears. He instructs the grenade launcher to open fire on the windows. However, both grenades go just above the roof and explode somewhere in the city. Rashid sends the driver with the keys from his office. He has four one-time American-made M-72 grenade launchers under my couch (my gift). Soon the driver returns with grenade launchers. Rashid neatly inserts into each window a shot. Hearing cries, smokes smoke. The sobered and frightened prince from the basement calls the MGB on the phone, asks him to stop the fire. He has a bunch of corpses. He surrenders. We visit Khoshal in the hospital. Two of his submachine gunners are on duty at his bunk and the doors of the ward around the clock. The prince managed to order his subordinates to liquidate the entire command of the operational regiment along with the advisers. He, of course, got excited. We have absolutely nothing to do with it. However, our leadership has sounded the alarm: - Fuck with them, with several advisers, it will be worse if we lose Kandahar! Prince can not be killed, you need to calm. He is immediately sent to Sochi with two faithful wives. With him, he took two bags of money: one with dollars, the other with checks of "outsourcing". For the sake of justice, it should be noted that the Pope-King publicly condemned the trick of his unreasonable offspring. Upon his return from the Union, the prince returned to his fiefdom, in Kandahar. However, the relatives of the murdered militants ran into the prince. He escaped the cordon. Soon he sent a tearful letter to one of the KGB advisers, invited him to ... Pakistan. Khoshal croaked quickly. Surprisingly, the 9 mm Makarov bullet passed between the trachea and the carotid artery, not catching the vital nerves, vessels and skeleton. Rashid was in the stupid role of the enemy's blood enemy yesterday's friend and drinking companion. We need to prepare for disassembly.

Yes, it used to be poisoned by guys who are older than campfires. Recently, that's what I told you. A narrow path, near by the aryk with water. Around thick thickets. After a few meters nothing is visible. The path winds and disappears behind the turn. An ambush in the forehead! A 50-meter piece detonating cord, to it every 5 meters attached hand grenade, only ten grenades. All this fits into the irrigation ditch with water and is masked from above by grass. This time we will carry out a detonation using an electric detonator. Garnet rings are pulled out. Their trigger arms are held by twine wrapped around the cord. By moving closer to the enemy's head patrol, the demoman presses the button. Explosion! How will a normal fighter do if he explodes half a meter from his leg? Naturally, he will fall on the path. If even the Mujahideen quickly come to their senses and start turning their heads around, it will be too late: grenades are lying around. Cracked grenade ruptures! Now our machine-gunner should be pressed to the ground of the remaining "spirits" with long bursts in the bushes, while the demolisher flees with all his might! Since in the "zelenok" head patrol moves at a distance of visibility (5-10 meters) from the core of the column, we with our minami-grenades necessarily catch from five to seventeen "spirits." Trophies are useless. Our task is to embed them properly, so that more of our street is not walking. For educational purposes we use grenades RGD-5. In the combat situation will have to put the F-1. The radius of solid defeat F-1 is 3.7 meters. For accuracy, reduce to 2, 5 meters. Therefore, if every five meters in the column of the enemy, even lying on the ground, explode on a grenade - will not seem a little. Tests on the testing range showed that a detonating cord can be thrown grenades F-1 at a distance of 0.5 to 15 meters.

I did not want to remember this much. From the words of one experienced warrior I'll tell you perhaps one bike. As the workload in the Pagmansky school increased, so did the number of accidents. However, without fatalities. Several times I got myself. The main reason for "PE" was that we used exclusively captured ammunition, often spoiled in dushman crude dumps (others I simply did not give). So, every third or fourth mine to the 82-mm mortar did not fire. Imagine the kind of hassle the extraction of a mine from the trunk turned into? Trouble was also brought to the Chinese RPG-2 grenade launcher. Their knock-out charges equipped with black, smoky powder, had a nasty property of dampening. So, twice instead of a shot, the gunpowder burned slowly, and the grenades, flying their trunks, fell a few meters from the gunner. One, thank God, did not explode. And the other, jerking, crooked splinters of three, including myself. This incident occurred during the second business trip. At the villa, soaping under the shower, I found on my body a lot of small acne, which somehow scraped palms. Looked closer - smaller pieces. I had to pick with a needle. A neighbor on the bunk, Alexander Ivanovich, seeing my belly garnished with belly, became alarmed: - Go to the doctor, sepsis may start! - Yeah, the doctor will report to the authorities, I will be arranged on a knuckle and will not be allowed to work. I found a large fragment in a leather purse, which was in my trouser pocket: it got stuck without breaking a thick pack of money - "afghani". If the fragment had hit the width of the palm to the right, it would have ended sadly. In this regard, Counselor Vitaly the next day, poisoned a fresh anecdote: - Beck returns from a business trip, the joyful spouse meets him, asks what gift he brought from Afghanistan? And sad Beck takes out of his pocket a dried man's nature on a string. Another reason for the accidents was that at the special forces school I was still preparing "supplies". The needs of the front required a large number of qualified fighters. They should not be afraid of close explosions and the whistle of bullets. As an example, I can compare the effectiveness of the work of two teachers sent from Moscow: for half a year my colleague, who was preparing pyrotechnics for a special laboratory, released ten Afghans. Over the same period I learned more than a hundred special forces. But this does not mean that their preparation was worse. Observant Afghans concluded: explosions are feared by either absolutely uninformed people or by professionals who know a lot. My students, it turns out, occupied an intermediate stage of nothing afraid of semi-professionals. What did not happen during the class. Once they shot at their feet from the AGS-17, another time a heavy trophy mine of 82-mm mortar fell in twenty meters from the gunmen. The cadet by mistake, instead of a regular knockout cartridge, was equipped with a mine by a cartridge of a hunting rifle of the 12th caliber. A nightmare episode occurred when an OGM-72 jumped mine landed in the hands of an Afghan! If it exploded, two thousand balls would turn us into mince! Fortunately, the mine was without a detonator capsule, so she just flew up into the air for about twenty meters and, slightly touching the Afghan tangent on her forehead, sent him to a deep knockout, and we, all sitting around, fell into a stupor for a few seconds. Cadets often received scratches, throwing hand grenades RGD-5 and RKG-3m standing. There were two explosions when they picked up unexploded ordnance at the site. The main thing at the training ground is to protect your eyes, you need to wear protective goggles. One unpleasant incident was remembered especially. I conducted classes on hand grenades with a group of employees of the special laboratories of the 5th UMGB. I have a lot of trophy grenades. All grenades are conditionally divided into two classes according to the type of fuse: instantaneous action and with a retarder, triggered a few seconds after the throw. The first type is our anti-tank RKG-3, another cumulative with a parachute, a plastic case in the form of a half liter bottle, or Chinese, or Pakistani production, our anti-personnel RGO and RGN, as well as a defensive grenade of a completely nightmarish design, Egyptian production. It was cylindrical, with an enameled body, wrapped in the middle of a thin elastic metal tape. It is pierced through with a check pin, the ring of the check has a lead seal. When you pull out a check, throw a grenade, in flight the elastic spring unwinds and flies off the body. When you touch the grenade on the surface, an explosion occurs. The most terrible thing is that under her body there are jagged metal gears, crushing during the explosion into many fragments, the size of a fingernail of a little finger. To the second class, I include our native F-1, RGD-5, RG-42, with fuses UZRGM, similar to them English, American and Belgian. I also include German ones with a long wooden handle and American M-46 with tear ignitors. So, he told the Afghan comrades about the grenade device, the rules for handling them and brought them to the training ground. I explain that with grenades of instantaneous action, you should handle cautiously, throw high and far, and immediately hide. With grenades of the second class, you can especially not stand on ceremony, for example, even F-1 does not throw, but simply lowered for a blow. One dumbass mixed up everything, and threw an Egyptian grenade of instant action nearby. She fell into the mud and did not explode, because the spring did not have time to unwind to the end. I warned the duty officer about the unworked grenade and instructed the sergeant to destroy her with an overhead charge. He did not. After lunch, an Afghan lieutenant went to those parts in need and, seeing an interesting toy, picked it up. A spring sprang to a click. He threw it aside. Explosion! As a razor, he was chopped off one foot to the knee. With the American grenade M-46 in the 40th Army there was a more sad case. One lieutenant-paratrooper decided that since she had a teething igniter like a rocket launcher, it was smoke. Seeing the soldier in the smoking room, he decided to play a trick, pulled the string and threw it into the middle. The soldiers did not stir. As a result, a dozen corpses. The officer shot himself. I had an average of one or two accidents a month. In this regard, it is difficult to describe your psychological state: most likely this can be called fatalism. And what else could I do? After all, it was in no way possible to show cadets timidity and fear. The instructor should be absolutely fearless person. In the end, gradually, fatigue accumulates, which even alcohol does not remove. Nervous breakdowns begin, sometimes completely unreasonable.

In vain reminded. This is a painful topic for many. But I will tell perhaps one story. The commander of the operational regiment (at that time our battalion grew up to the regiment) invites advisers to the holiday "Kurban-Mayram". But the leadership, fearing possible excesses, does not allow participating in the Muslim holiday. The Afghans are sulking at us. The regiment's regiment laments: "We bought snacks for 20,000 afghanis, and you did not come!" This is a mortal offense. Serega instructs me as a Muslim to settle the incident. Besides, at this time I was on a business trip in the provinces, so I did not seem to have anything to do with the incident. I swear to the partners that the advisers have nothing to do with it, and that we are also worried about what happened. We are ready to atone for our guilt. The next day the whole group come to the regiment. In a car - a "pill", except for a few liters of cognac, vodka, champagne and a box of dry wine, we carry several different trunks. At the regimental shooting range, spread out the cloak-tents. They lay a German machine gun MG-42, nicknamed "Hitler's saw," a PK machine gun, an American M-16 rifle, as well as our PKK, AKS-74. Two soldiers-translators stuff horns and equip machine-gun belts. After three obligatory toasts, I announce a competition: a drink for someone who hits the target! The Afghans are trying: the lines are rattling. Gradually, all enter into excitement. With my side vision, I notice that Dean Mamad, as soon as the shooting begins, quietly creeps back. And while all compete in accuracy, he manages to overturn an extra glass, obligingly substituted by an interpreter, and crawl back to the firing line. We all like MG-42: the rate of fire is 1300 per minute and the almost complete absence of dispersion: for 800 meters in a small target, the turn is put! Shooting ammunition and lakirnuv champagne, in an embrace with partners, we return to the headquarters. The incident is over. Dean Mamad - a large, slightly stooped fellow, arrived in the battalion of the "commandos", ruschisch - rake to the knees, buzzing in my ear: he offers to go to Kabul to the girls. He's got a good "chicken coop" somewhere. However, he only has the strength to get to his bunk. So it is cut off, the poor fellow, with his eyes open. I close my eyes: "Sleep well, friend Dean Mamad ..."

One friend recently told a story. As soon as we entered the control sector of the Chechen militants, she recalled their commander and spoke for a long time, pointing to me. Then she said to me: "They thought you were an agent of the FSB, they wanted to shoot him or take him to the mountains, but I defended you." Of course, I crumbled in thanks, but if you think logically - who could call me a security officer? Maybe she just did not believe it or did not have time to deal with me? Her son named Ruslan after the year 96 gladly helped me to travel throughout Ingushetia - first on my "nine", then on the sports "Porsche". Talking with him was not about anything, except about women, but for work very convenient. Whatever is, but still familiar. "Brother" ... On the eve of October 4, when he volunteered with a breeze to throw up a checkpoint at Mozdok, I was struck by one thing. Ruslan told that he has a beautiful girlfriend, whom he goes to almost every night, and then reported that he is married and has two children. I asked: "Does the wife know? What does she say?" He replied calmly: "What can I say to her? She cries." In this calmness was such cruelty that I felt uneasy.

I heard something. I'll tell you from the mouth of an eyewitness. In the morning of October 4, I overslept. Having barely rinsed, he grabbed his belongings and went down to the hotel lobby. Ruslan was already there. It was embarrassing for me that he had to wait, I fussed, leaving the hotel and paying for all telephone calls. Neither when we circled around Nazran and Ruslan ran about his affairs to different addresses, or on the route Nazran-Malgobek, I did not remember the passport forgotten by the hotel's administrators. And no suspicions arose. Anyway, I would be in Mozdok for lunch and maybe even have time to be accredited in the press center of a group of federal troops preparing to throw into Chechnya. With luck at all could be in the evening in the position of some advanced unit. With luck ... Most likely the decision to "sell" me came from Ruslan only upon his return to Nazran, when he again started calling at different addresses. I even imagine such a dialogue: "I have a journalist sitting in my car ..." "Yes, who?" "Like from the Moscow News." "One?" "Yes ..." "Wait, wait ..." Ak ... Go to Magomed home, tell him everything, let your guys quickly and quickly collect. And then you will go to the hospital, you will find Said. He will pay you back. With him decide where to place ... "Ruslan worked his money well - I did not even think of anything like that. The day was warm and sunny in summer and I handed over one of the materials, and he was put in the room, there were five more days ahead of him, so there was a chance to excel and do something for the newspaper. In general, the mood was excellent ...

Yes, it used to be poisoned by guys who are older than campfires. The narrative will be from the words of an eyewitness. The first days in captivity, the moment of capture, I recalled with horror and pain. Stupid, bastard cattle, greedy creatures, imagining themselves supermen! After all, they were already bombed with terrible force, the federal troops were brought to full combat readiness, and the situation did not in any way suggest kidnapping - this is not the 97th year when the bandits were simply besieged with impunity, stealing journalists one by one! After all, the federal troops are what they are, but not the rabble, called Maskhadov's "presidential guard" and "subdivisions of the Ministry of Shariah state security!" No matter how the Russian army decomposes, it is even impossible to imagine that its officers themselves took part in abductions of strangers, As did the Chechen bandits in the uniforms of sovereign Ichkeria! Now, remembering that terrible moment when the Judas left me alone in his car on the orders of the republican hospital, and himself went allegedly to his beautiful girlfriend, and the inconspicuous white "six" with the Ingush numbers stopped nearby, I understand that such a noiseless and impetuous The operation became possible only because I was confused for a split second. If I managed to yell with good mate or at least start a fight, then everything would have turned out differently. After all, just around the corner of the hospital, there was a busy place - a bus stop, several shopping pavilions. This completely relaxed me, and when a blond man in military uniform without shoulder straps peeped in the door of the Porsche with the prudent Judas lowered his glass: "Your documents! Checking the passport regime, "I again did not think anything wrong." Just got out of the car and went to the white "six" ...

I did not want to remember this much, but it was. It is our life. History told me this, my former partner. I will pass from his words. Pushing me into the back seat of his "six", the bandits wheezed in the ear: "Shut up, bitch! I was going to kill myself !!! ", for the sake of persuasiveness in the side I was painfully poked with barrels of pistols." In the cellophane bag, the air ran out very quickly, and I began to choke, especially since I constantly tried to scream. "From that moment, time crumpled to me like a piece of paper. Remember that the machine instantly surged through the streets of Nazran, someone's sure hands tore c me napojasnuju handbag, where were all the documents and money and wrist watch "komandirskie" gift of mother, I swam with scuba diving in Crete in the year 98, someone forces from all over still clutched my head to her knees, and somewhere in the outside world, beyond the package causing thugs talking jerky phrases on the Chechen or Ingush language. I remember, from the shock, I shouted all sorts of nonsense: "Basayev will kill you for me! Release or regret! Well, please do not shoot! Then kill me, do not carry anywhere!" The bandit poking at me in the side with a pistol shouted: "Another word - I swear, I'll shoot like a dog, right here!" - and hit the handle of the gun somewhere above the ear. Slightly, but very painful. Very soon I was weak from lack of air. He gasped and now no longer required, but begged to give a little breathe. The car was still racing at full speed and, as far as I can tell now, all the time on the asphalt surface. Somehow croaked: "You are Chechens! Better cut, do not smother!" One gangster replied: "We are not Chechens. We are Arabs, "and all four were booed with hideous, vile, deliberately vile voices, but the edge of the package was lifted, and the air of the car's saloon, stinking with the bodies of five sweaty peasants, seemed refreshing like a sea breeze. Then they put him in another car: "Get out quickly, bitch, I'll kill you! Come here!" - And then in the third. Bandits everywhere changed. In the second car they were "kind" - they raised the package so that I could breathe not only with my mouth, but with my nose, and gave a smoke. In the third car there were again "bad", silent and embittered. I remember the softness of the earth when I was put under a fruit tree ("Yeah, it's not far from Assinovskaya!" Once there was a fruit and berry state farm, Salam Aleykum, Chechnya ... "I lay face down, arms and shoulders from the ropes were already stiffened, And two submachine gunners were forbidden even to move, so as to somehow reduce the unbearable pain in their shoulders. In five minutes or an hour after the trees, the sound of an approaching car was heard, the bandits kicked me up (with incredible difficulty getting up with twisted arms) and again drove off like a ram, accompanied by senseless evil cries. It was already dark when I was brought to zindan. My new residence (for a short while, just for two weeks, as I found out later) was an unfinished garage among the ruins and piles of broken bricks. Then I could not believe that just a few hours ago I was free, I could plan further steps and "swing the rights" if someone wants to interfere. Now I can not believe that I was a hostage - that is, not entirely human and not even a person - and that any of the bandits, with their own understanding and mood, would do absolutely anything to me with impunity.

In vain reminded. This is a painful topic for many. That's what our commander told us. Now, when a wave of panic horror, pain and constant tension subsided, I often think: but could it be otherwise? After all, all the circumstances that led to that fatal moment on October 4, 1999, at about 15-16 pm, when four bandits forcibly took me away in their car, now look like a chaotic set of accidents and nonsense. Now, if on the eve of the day I suddenly decided not to visit the friends of Nazran ... If the hotel porter of the Assa hotel were not so nice and friendly girls who did not want to offend distrust (two days before the seizure I went up to them and jokingly asked: "And my passport will not be lost to you?" The girls got agitated and Offered to return it to me, to which I replied: "No, no, you will have it wholeheartedly - I always lose everything") ... If it were not for my damned forgetfulness ... If the Judas by the name of Ruslan had some old "Zhiguli", and not a sports double "Porsche", where you can get to Mozdok quickly and comfortably ... From these " If "there is any business trip - it does not matter, in the" hot spot "or in a prosperous country. But no matter how many trips I had during the years of work in the Moscow News, the circumstances always developed as best as possible. Probably, the limit of luck and luck was exhausted and on that last trip to Ingushetia on the eve of a new big war something bad would necessarily happen. Happiness, that it was "only" capture by the hostages. Happiness, that always, even on the darkest days, there was a tiny hope.

One friend recently told a story. More precisely, this is not even a story but an outline of eyewitnesses. Remembering now the people who knew me from the year 96, who called the "nephew" and "brother" and coolly directed the bandits against me, I feel a quiet, quiet disappointment, besides the thirst for vengeance and hatred. Not so much in their decency, as in their mind. What a stupid and greedy person one must be in order not to masquerade and not sweep the tracks, to bring me into the clutches of the bandits! Back in zindan I found only two explanations for such perfidy - or they themselves considered me to be a complete fool who could not draw the simplest conclusions, or they were told that I would not stay alive. The second explanation became bad. I will not name "gunners" - mother and son, although their name has already sounded in the "Moscow News". This couple is now sitting in the remand center, and the story of my captivity is just a small episode of the turbulent activity of a fun family. Mother even earlier, in the year 96, looked somewhat suspicious - too businesslike, "bolted", whether that, she then was, with extensive connections in international humanitarian organizations and among Chechen fighters. She told that she had an unselfish and noble nature, that at the call of the soul she was engaged in the release of Russian prisoners of war, that in this difficult matter she had repeatedly looked into death's eyes. For the sake of justice, it should be noted that she really was not too afraid when the two of us arrived on March 7, 96 in Grozny, engrossed in battles. Then I did not understand why she needed it.

I heard something. More precisely read the last essays of the former commander. Here, listen. On April 4 at about 15.00 the gunner of the BMP pushed us: "Get up, let's go soon!" And really, in 15 minutes we moved forward ... 600 meters to the road we crossed an hour and a half. The spirits were higher and shot us like in a dash. On the road from our company came 2nd and 3rd platoons, and the 1st and company control remained at the position two hundred meters behind, the 2nd and 6th companies went around Goyskoye to the left. We decided to go for the road, asked to cover up, but I do not shoot SVD: a grenade splinter fell into the bolt. I have disassembled the rifle. In the receiver, there were still some glands, it is also visible fragments. I checked everything, I tried the rifle - nothing, it works. Our group went after the road, pelting grenades with grenades. First, a pair of machine-gunner mounted on the spot. And the others left under cover. We beat the spirits out of position. Apparently, there were about twenty of them. Leaving, they left five for cover. These unfortunate grenades were blown to shreds. Did not help them and dug under the road holes. We are entrenched. At this time, the 6th and 2nd companies fought a fierce battle in the "green". Eleven people, together with the platoon, who performed the duty of the company, lay down there. Two weeks later, the spirits were asked to pick up the corpses, since they had already decayed. But it's later, but for now we blocked the right flank of the 2nd and 6th mouth. In one of the burrows a live "Czech" was discovered, which managed to overwhelm the kid who found him. No matter how we tried to smoke a Czech, it was all wasted. We poured kerosene into the hole, and set fire to it, and threw grenades. Apparently, the course in the shelter was cranked, so it did not get ... While the court is yes, we look, the main forces of the company have approached. It turns out that they, having lost four, could not overcome the field. Our platoon mate with the company about their late approach. Mouth shouts: "There were no clear instructions from the battalion commander!" Platoon: "The battalion commander was drunk in the trash." It was necessary to make the decision himself! " While they were understanding, we inspected the Chechen trenches and dug-outs, finished off the wounded. After a while, the co-manda: "Leave!" That this is nonsense, it is clear to everyone. One of the conscripts-demobilists tried to explain by the radio station supposedly to be fixed, because if not we, then the spirits will come here, and again we will have to seize the position with losses. He shouted hoarsely, with matyugami, but, of course, nobody could convince. Having provided the output of the 6th company, they began to withdraw. SAU from 324 msp began to hollow Goiskoe, and we divided into two groups. The first departs with all the belongings, the second closes. We departed normally as a whole, but it was not without jokes. The last tank left the number 420. He covered all, "to the heap." During the assault, the spirits were burned by two infantry fighting vehicles: one of our, one of the 6th company. To be faithful, the tank pierced the padded BMP. And then some kind of gunner-operator from the "two" on it as sharahnet! Tankmen then said: "We are, of course, in fact, but when you retire your own they are hammered in the ass ..." Who fired, they did not find out.

Yes, it used to be poisoned by guys who are older than campfires. One of them had a military campaign in the past. That's what he told them. Dirty and boring, around scurrying newcomers from some sort of a brigade airborne troops. For three days together with the paratroopers we made an impregnable fortress out of Khankala. Now it's a little calmer. The spirits stopped their ridiculous forays at night, we were no longer chased about and without reason. Yes, we have cowardly generals, a whole brigade of VV and a battalion of paratroopers have been assigned to guard the tiny Khankala, they also promise to send a regiment of motorized rifles. Where they will only fit? But this is all the lyrics, and there is nothing to do at the moment. I go to the radio operators, you need to be aware of the events, or the war will end and you will not know. - It's great "Broom". - A "Moscow" come in, do you smoke? "Let's find what's new?" - Yes, nothing, Marines, landing party, motorized rifles, and Sofrintsy peck A minute ago Dudayev asshole is not well-known, shorter life is shit, and the sun is fucked lamp! "Have you heard that we can hear from our brigade?" - Nothing concrete, we wait, along the way we guard the "shakal" command... Trying to configure another wave on the two-way radio, crackling ether interrupted "Chukotka" laryngeal speech, one Russian, not hell sucks go further, find a rifle part talks they clamped "spirits" in the five-story building, no callsigns and TP, boric mate from which left that kind of Ensign Zinoviev had sexual intercourse with one of BMPS in place that it's difficult to imagine on bmp ... A good stretch thing, a regular campaign of spirits behind our "beps" was noticed on time. Well-fed "Broom" during my radio interceptions had time to "sleep" and under-jumping probably half a meter, he snuggled off his chair crawling along the kunga trying to simultaneously attach the armor, the pouch for the grenades eventually he stumbled on the helmet and fell wildly to the floor with a wild crash. And it would be very funny, if it were not so ne-pawalno. In a matter of seconds I reach the technics with the eyes of a platoon commander, he agitated behind a piece of armature and with great enthusiasm hrenachit on dusman who, by virtue of natural dullness, climbed out onto an open site. Toli we are in truth a not-bad army, and not a flock of deer as the battalion commander explained to us in the "training", toli the fools fell to us like dumb ones, but we kept Khankala on conscience and there were almost no losses in personnel. Two "two hundredth" in six days in January 95 in Chechnya is not bad, although it's bad, very bad because it could not be them either. A few minutes later we collected a combined platoon of kol-vo near the office and went to the web, naturally there were no spirits in the neighborhood any more, we put several stretches and "lanterns" for not a horse-radish without our knowledge in the district to fumble. Another day has passed, on another war ... Who needs all this ...

I did not want to remember this much. This is from the words of an eyewitness. After passing militant guards in Grozny, we penetrated into the quarters, completely controlled by "spirits." Army camouflage and our unshaven, blackened, cheeky faces gave us a chance to completely descend for the militants in case of a sudden encounter with the "soldiers of Ichkeria." The mess in the militants was analogous to the brothel that reigned in the disposition of our troops. The system of omissions and passwords in the conditions of general confusion among the militants sometimes did not work, and it was enough to shout "Allah Akbar", so that they would take us for their own. Our goal was to conduct reconnaissance in the territory behind militant militant orders and disorganize the enemy communications system by mining to introduce a sense of chaos and panic into its ranks. They moved only in the evening, but before dark. With the onset of darkness, advancement through the city is dangerous, as at this time the belligerents doubled the attention behind all actions from the contiguous side. At night we sat in smelly cellars or in abandoned private houses, which we selected after preliminary reconnaissance so that all approaches to our shelter could be clearly seen. It's already getting dark. We are circling in the area for the second day, we can not find a passage to the neighboring region, teeming with bearded men with green armbands armed militants. It is necessary to sit out this night, and at dawn go to the next street. I noticed this afternoon that the militants designated on our map had been turned into a stronghold for a breakdown of Russian tanks, having overwhelmed it with various large-sized trash consisting of broken cars and a half-burned bus. The sixth sense told me that for sure this improvised barricade is "guarded" by the "spirits," therefore, one should not fool around there. After exposing the guard and the observer, we lay down to rest on the pile of broken brick. Bricks stick in the back, and internal tension does not allow to fall asleep. Turning on my stomach, lay down on bent hands, - the posture is not comfortable, but I force myself to fall asleep, tomorrow is a difficult day. It can not be called sleep. Closing your eyes, you fall into a trance of forgetting, continuing to somehow control the environment. I came to myself suddenly. Silence. However, in the stomach it became cold and uncomfortable. A second later he heard a crunch of garbage, heavy footsteps, and saw carefully the roaming beam of a torchlight. "Have you spotted, where is the observer, is it really already lying with a cut throat?" - my condition was close to panic. I can not hear my companions breathing next to me. So they do not sleep, and just like me, hiding, they squeeze into a pile of bricks. People with a flashlight (probably it was a patrol of militants) stopped near the entrance to our shelter. There was a silence, which seemed to me a lifetime. His heart rolled to his throat. In the morning I read "Our Father," and God will save me, "- this thought, if not soothing, at any rate makes me get rid of the paralyzing animal fear of the unknown and prepare for action. The machine is at hand. The fingers of the other hand grope for the cold ribbed surface of the F-1 grenade, the purpose of which is to not be captured alive. The steps began to move away. "Have not found". The tension subsided. However, the alarm for the guys who were on the watch did not pass. A conditional signal. "Our!" Two figures, like shadows, slipped to us. As it turned out, the gunmen appeared suddenly from some basement, to which we at first did not even pay attention. When it began to dawn, we cautiously left our shelter, having previously left several "guests" in the form of two MON-50. Coming out of the basement and looking around, they found an unobtrusive passage to the basement, which later turned out to be a through passage to the street where we could not get to before. Having mined this passage, which the fighters are likely to try to use, our group leaked out behind the "spirits" in the area of ??the tram park. We did not have much time to linger here. The depot of the tram park and the adjacent territory were swarming with militants, since Maskhadov's headquarters were here, and the militants could run into our "guests" and realize that other people have appeared on their territory. Collecting intelligence through visual observation, trying to quickly get rid of cargo - installing and masking mines, we began to prepare for a retreat. The departure route, developed by us and coordinated with the command of the units, to the border of which we had to leave after the operation, turned out to be useless. Rather, it will be said that by this time, while we were in the rear of the enemy, the situation has changed. The frontiers of contact with the enemy moved, in some directions the militants created new strong points. Based on the current situation, we had to conduct additional exploration and look for new escape routes.

In vain reminded. This is a painful topic for many. Listen to the essay of a veteran. Despite the fact that in our group there were children who lived up to all these events in Grozny, it was very difficult to navigate in a ruined city. In some places five days ago there were enemy positions, and now they can be occupied by ours, as a result of which there is a high possibility of getting under federal fire. Although there are no "smart" deaths, it is foolish to get a bullet from your own. To prevent this from happening, we had to constantly conduct additional reconnaissance of the area. As a witness of the consequences, when our reconnaissance groups attacked their own, I became in January 1995. Similar to our reconnaissance group under the command of Vadim flew to the "Volgo-Grad" (part of the 8th Army Corps under the command of General Rokhlin) in one of the districts of Grozny. The lack of interaction played a tragic role, the Vadim group was accepted by the "Volgograds" for the enemy, and fire was opened on it for defeat. One of the group was killed. Vadim jumped out of the hiding place and started shouting: "Do not shoot, we are ours!" After he was "detained" run up fighters, it turned out that he had no documents. Trying to explain himself to the "Volgograd" Vadim was severely beaten and almost shot. After clarifying all the circumstances of the case and asking for confirmation to the higher headquarters, Vadim was critically put in a hospital at Severniy. I was standing by his bed, and it hurt to listen to his jerky, stuttering speech, in which he only often repeated: "T-t-you-p-p-pee-pee, I-I in Af-fgane p- To-kon-tuziyu, and z-z-here m-me to-to-the-end ... "Two hours later a special turn-key was brought to Mozdok, and then to the hospital on the" big earth ". Having run a small square, through the arch we jump into the courtyard. He seems to be from another life. The war spared him so much that the ruins on the neighboring streets give the impression that the arch through which we entered this courtyard was a gate from one world to another. Silence is unfamiliar. The fact that silence in the war is sometimes deceptive, we made sure in a few minutes. Having trusted in the silence and apparent calm, we made a mistake that almost led to a tragic ending for us. We violated the basic rules of movement on the territory occupied by the enemy, which read: "Do not believe your eyes and ears. Trust only the instinct and instinct." Seven times check ... "and other principles according to which we have no right to relax. From the window of the second floor of the neighboring house we were called in Chechen. We are confused ... Not having received an answer, they opened fire on us. But either the arrows turned out to be fucking, or because these "animal instincts" awoke in us again, only one person from our group was slightly injured in the side, and then tangential, as we immediately found ourselves behind high concrete slabs, Hiding from enemy fire. In order not to waste ammunition in vain, we decided not to carry out an intensive targeted shooting on the enemy who was in the building, and, consequently, in a favorable position, especially since we were not aware of their forces and intentions. Being under the cover of these same concrete slabs, snapping short bursts and single fire, we tried to gain time for the adoption of the very "only correct decision." Through the windows of the basement, we entered one by one into the three-story house behind our backs. Cursing and swearing for carelessness, the first thing they did was help the wounded. Two went on scouting. The rest took up defense. Returning from intelligence brought a joyful message: in some distance from us are the lines of the Russian troops storming the city. Apparently, the immediate proximity of the federal troops did not allow the militants who fired at us in the courtyard to take more decisive action against our group, who settled in this three-story house. Or maybe it was similar to our enemy group, whose task is not to conduct a protracted open battle. In any case, we quickly established contact with the command of the federal troops, to the frontier of which came out, and within an hour we were sent to the Northern Fleet on armored personnel carriers.

One friend recently told a story. Now I'll tell you. "Armistice" the next morning turned into a bloody tragedy. At 10 o'clock in the morning on Staropromyslovskoe highway, militants undermined the landmine installed on the roadside. Splinters of a 152-mm howitzer projectile (tanks and howitzer shells used by militants as the main landfill bomb) penetrated armored personnel carriers, instantly killing the driver-mechanic. The unguided BTR at a speed of 70 km / h, jumped to the opposite side of the highway and cut off the concrete column of illumination, stopped, filled with blood and spattered by the brains of servicemen of the 101st BB brigade. Four soldiers died in a matter of seconds, the fifth, who was torn apart by the explosion of his legs, died on the way to the hospital, the sixth died on the operating table. Near the place of the tragedy was an elderly Russian woman, who covered her face with the cloak of the cloak, unable to hide her tears. She has to hide her tears from the crowd of Chechen youth gathered at a crossroads and cheerfully discussing what happened. On the same day, five-fighters captured at one of the roadblocks were taken to GUOSH. Dressed in black clothes, more like uniforms, they carried weapons, half of which were equipped with devices for noiseless shooting. You-coming from Shali after a federal operation there under the leadership of General Shamanov in May 1996, this group tried to penetrate Grozny. The senior group of militants, as it turned out during the investigation, was simultaneously a commander of a reconnaissance group from the detachment of Shamil Basayev. Captured militants have shown that they arrived in Grozny to carry out terrorist attacks. This was evidenced by the list of persons included in the government of Chechnya found with them, indicating the positions and locations of the working classrooms. During the interrogations it was found out that the militants had also been ordered to take out from Grozny, prior to June 10, the family and relatives of militants fighting in the mountains. All this gave grounds to believe that militants use "truces" for their own purposes, and they are far from peaceful. The atmosphere in Chechnya was getting worse every day. The unwillingness of the military and political leadership of Russia to live according to the laws of the not-declared, but still war, led to the tragic events in Grozny on August 6,

I heard something. Just here one story was remembered. It has long been true. My company was raised to disarm one Chechen gadyushnik, and the "Vovan" (special forces of the Interior Ministry) worked, and we only closed it. When the elders of the village were brought to the commander of the Vovans, he demanded 24 AKs to be handed over to them within two hours. On which one of the elders began fucking in the style of Lukashenka. He said that in their village there is a legitimate detachment of self-defense, but they do not have enough automatic weapons, so the feds are obliged to immediately issue another 20 AK. Vovany from such borzoty slightly prizhuhli, but we are not confused. The ugly elder in front of everyone received a turn in the bread-cutter, and while his legs were still twitching, the other elders heard a delicate request to hand over not 24 AK and 100. And not for two hours, but for an hour. The Chechens met in forty minutes, handing over exactly 100 automatic rifles. And the moral of this story is this: politics and diplomacy are good for a partner who remains within certain limits. For the "partner" who lost the shore, there must be a different set of tools. I saw the columns of buses, to which because of the stench could not be approached for a hundred meters, because they were stuffed with bodies of slaughtered Russians. I saw women, ditch-nenki sawn along the chainsaw, kids, planted on poles from traffic signs, artistically wound on the fence of the intestine. We, Russians, have been cleansed from our own land like dirt from under our nails. And it was 1992 - until the "first Chechen" remained two and a half years. And then it became quite fun. The village came militants and began to clean it from the Russians. At night, people could be heard shouting and being raped and slaughtered in their own house. And no one came to their aid. Everyone was for himself, everyone was shaking with fear, and some managed to bring the ideological base to this matter, they say, "my house is my fortress" (yes, this phrase I heard at that time. The man who uttered it is no longer alive - his Vainakhi intestines were wound on the fence of his own house). That's how we, cowardly and stupid, were cut out singly. Tens of thousands of Russians were killed, several thousand were slaves and Chechen harems, hundreds of thousands fled Chechnya in their underpants. So the Vainakhs solved the "Russian question" in a single republic. And they succeeded only because we were insignificant, full of shit. We're still shit, though it's not that liquid anymore - steel pellets began to fall among the shit. And when these grains come together - there are kondopogi. There are not many of them yet, but Vainakhs are good fellows. Real orderlies of the forest. As a result of their cultural and educational mission in Russia, Russian sheep again become human beings. In general, those who crossed paths with Chechens have something to hate.

Yes, it used to be poisoned by guys who are older than campfires. A couple of veterans had a hike there. Here, listen. In the same place, in the war, fate brought me with one Jew - Lev Yakovlevich Rokhlin. Initially, our participation in the New Year's storm was not expected. But when we lost contact with the 131st MSR and the 81st MSP, we were thrown to the rescue. We broke through to the location of the 8 AK, which was commanded by General Rokhlin, and arrived at his headquarters. Then I first saw him in person. And he did not seem to me at first sight: hunched, chilled, with cracked glasses ... Not a general, but some tired agronomist. He set us the task of collecting the scattered remains of the Maikop brigade and the 81st regiment and leading them to the pvd of the Rokhlinsky intelligence battalion. This is what we were doing - we collected meat from the fears and took them to the location of the Rokhlin scouts. In total there were about two mouths. Initially, Rokhlin did not want to use them, but when all the other groups retreated - 8 AK remained alone in the operational environment in the center of the city. Against all militants! And then Rokhlin built this "army" opposite the structure of his fighters and addressed them with a speech. I will never forget this speech. The gentle words of the general were: "fucking monkeys" and "piedaras." In the end, he said: "The militants outnumber us fifteen times, and we can not expect help from anybody, and if we are to lie down here, let each of us be found under a bunch of enemy corpses." Let's show how the Russian fighters and the Russians are able to die Generals, do not fail, my sons ... " Leo Yakovlevich is no longer alive - he was sorted out without you. One Jew is less, is not it? I already told you about Rokhlin's speech. But I did not tell you about what happened next. And then there was a terrible, terrible battle, in which of my platoon of 19 people left six alive. And when the Chechens broke into the location and it came to grenades, and we realized that we all get fucked up - I saw real Russian people. Fear was gone. There was some kind of merry anger, detachment from everything. There was one thought in my head: "Dad" asked me not to let him down. "The wounded themselves bandaged themselves, cheated with promedol and continued the fight. Then the Vainakh and I met in hand-to-hand combat. And they ran. This was a turning point in the battle for Grozny. It was a confrontation between two characters - Caucasian and Russian, and ours was harder. It was at that moment that I realized that we could do this. This solid core in us is, it only needs to be cleared of the adherent shit. We took prisoners in hand-to-hand combat. Looking at us, they did not even whine - they howled in horror. And then we read the radio interception - on the radio networks of the militants, Dudayev's order was passed: "scouts from the 8K and special forces of the Airborne Forces are not taken prisoner and tortured, but immediately finished off and buried as soldiers." We were very proud of this order.

I did not want to remember this much. Troubled time was then. Between Goysky and Komsomolsky - gardens, and behind them the spirits of the position of ATGM. The operator was classy, ??they got three versts in a lagoon for three. Moreover, he tried to cover KP 324 th regiment, fired at the roadblock, and before him all four kilometers. This ATGM was aimed at us by a battalion commander. We proceeded from the assumption that the position is well guarded, and if the operator falls, then noiselessly. Therefore, they asked the battalion commander to write out Vintorez from the warehouse of the RAV VSS. He ordered the rifle, but someone had to go with her along with the chief of the missile and artillery armament service. In the regiment, we decided to go on a passing column. The situation in Goi is as follows: the spirits could not break the confrontation in their direction, since we surpassed them in fire weapons (artillery, aviation, etc.), and we could not take Goiskoye because of its small numbers. Anyway, we got the spirits, and they told us that if any other unit of 245 msp were to go to Goisky, they would block the gorge and block the regiment. In the Lower Atagi, where we waited for the column, she came at half past two. In its composition were to follow in the regiment of conscript-conscripts from the raiding party, as well as those who went to take out on leave for family reasons. (Naturally, they, like me, were not taken into account anywhere, and therefore later, when the battle was over, the exact number of losses in our ill-fated column was difficult to calculate. In particular, the "Ural" with the demobilization, which was a man of 20, was burnt after one hit "Bumblebee". They carried food, and the boys sat on bags from above - so everything was burnt ...) I went through the column to find out about the mail - there were no letters. I go back, I look - four consoles in a row, and one of them has a good friend and countryman Arkasha. It turned out that he was a deputy of the platoon of the liquidators. Well, lucky! "Arkasha, there is an empty seat in the cockpit?" It did not fit the sniper - the white man - to shake in the armor on the armor. " He says: "Come, look!" He went in, pushed a packet of vodka, which he was carrying to someone for his birthday. Nothing, I'll fit myself. At about 2 pm we started off. At 14.10 Chishki passed and before the entrance to the gorge they jerked the gates. Arkasha says: "Look, some women and children." And I literally yesterday guys from the 324th regiment said: "If the road guys, women and children - everything is fine .If some women - cranks, soon an ambush." The column stretched out on the "mother-in-law language" (this is a serpentine). On it, the fins were barely unfolding, and the MAZs, which were pulling the faulty machinery, do not know how they passed at all. Everything is quiet, calm. We're going, we're jamming the jokes. As it turned out later, Khattab planned everything so competently that, literally at the very beginning of the battle, all the controls that were traveling on the two command cars were cut by the fire of small arms, and the KSM itself remained untouched during the whole battle. Suddenly in the second Ural with high-explosive ammunition something exploded so that the rear axle with one wheel of a candle for 80 meters went up, and, according to our reasons, it was to fall flat on us. Well, we think, have come. But lucky: he fell about ten meters. Everything in the smoke, everything explodes. Because of the smoke, nothing is visible in sight. The shooting is erratic, but the machine gunner of spirits stood out against the general background. We decided to get out of this hell, get out, ran into the "zelenok". Distributed with Dima sectors of the shelling. I am firing along the front, and he covers my rear and looks to the spirits from above did not go. Crawled to the edge, and the tank, which in the tail of the column stood, the spirits of the RPG grind. Once eight got, but to no avail. Then they broke through the tower from the side of the commander's hatch. Smoke poured from her. Apparently, the crew was wounded, and the mechanic began to take back. So backwards he went through the whole column and, they say, got to the regiment. An hour passed since the beginning of the battle. The shooting began to subside. I say: "Well, everything, Dima, pull at the end of the column!" They ran under the bridge, I look, some people are sitting in the "Afghan", seven people, next to two dead bodies. We run up. One of the seated turns. Oh my God! He has a black beard, a nose with a hump and wild eyes. I throw up my rifle, press on the descent ... The others turn around-ours. Well, I did not finish it. The bearded contractor turned out to be. He without me, stunned, sits, stammers, can not say anything. I shout: "Uncle, I almost knocked you down!" And he does not cut it. In our side the BMP "limping" creeps, picking up the wounded. She was in a torsion, and she stumbles. They threw the wounded inside, drove to the road - around the car burned out, something is torn in them. The skirmish almost subsided. Let's go. Somewhere closer to Argun on the road, the peasants shout: "Guys, we have wounded people here. Help!" I jumped to them, and the car went on. I go up to the guys. They say: "We have a major wounded." The major is in camouflage, with the sign of the Marine Corps on his sleeve. End-to-end wound in the arm and in the chest. All pale from the loss of blood. The only thing that I had was a tourniquet. I stretched out my hand to him. They talked, it turned out that he was a deputy battalion of the battalion in the Pacific Fleet. At this time, one of the guys remembered that they were carrying beer, cigarettes, juice, etc. in the car. I covered the guys, and they ran off bringing all this good. We lie, drink beer, smoke. Darken the beginning. I think: "It's getting dark now, the spirits will come down, there's no help, and we are shaking!" We decided to choose a better position. Have fondled пригорочек, have borrowed it, we lay, we wait. The guys from the RMS show me the situation. Machines with ammunition spirits were burned from RPGs, and those with food were simply sowing from small arms. The artillery was working, very carefully, only on the slopes, and not touching the settlement or us. Then came four MI-24s, worked on the mountains. It's getting darker. We hear, from the side of the 324th regiment - an eerie roar. It turns out that the help rolls. Ahead of the T-72, behind him the infantry fighting vehicle, then again the tank. Not reaching 50 meters, he stops and points us at the gun. I think: "Everything! The spirits did not crash - they will finish their own with fright!" We jumped up, hands mash - they say, their own. The tank shook the barrel, turned around and as sharahnet in the "zelenok" 20 meters away. With this "help" people povyzkakivalo - on the grass creep around itself from submachine guns watered. We told them: "Guys, are you creeping? There's no one there." It turns out that it was the reconnaissance of the 324th regiment. I approached the officers, I said: "Why are you fighting here? You have to go to the head of the column!" And they told me: since you've been here before, and think, take ten people and move with them, where he himself said. I was like, I found the scouts, and we moved forward. I counted more than forty burnt corpses. Judging by what cars were left intact, the spirits had clear information that where is located. For example, the medical MTLB in general remained untouched, only the mechanics of small arms were filled up, and Zuushka was literally turned into a sieve. Then we asked why the help came so late: if they had come an hour and a half early, then someone would have survived in the column's head, and so up to the last one BRDM resisted, in which almost all were killed. As the guys from the 324th regiment later told us, when they reported that they were wetting our column in the gorge and would have been good to rush to the rescue, they were told not to jerk and stand where they stood. Help came to us after two and a half hours, when it was all over.

In vain reminded. This is a painful topic for many. It was found in the diaries of one of the dead. Heck! If I get out, I'll quit smoking, drinking, and the like, but later! Now we must hold out! Then, as if on command, the fire from the spirits ceased. The mines stopped whistling, the ricochet stalled, and the "aboriginal" cries were not heard. "What is it?" - Who knows! - Guys are desantura! Exactly the elite! No one else! - Hooray! "Damn the devil!" Without a team, without anything, they all rose from the fortifications as one and rushed to the unpretentious two or three story buildings. And then a whole minibigade of the "fathers of commanders" and ... Quiet (as far as possible in Chechnya) winter evening, I sit on the steps of the new headquarters, smoke and I think that finally it all ended. We are BB - it means cops, and the matter of cops is guarded. So Grozny is going to drop the landing party, but I'm done, fought! Six days later our brigade was sent to Grozny ...

Well, listen. At the university at a lecture on psychology. Professor: - Today we will study, on an example, three initial stages of the human psyche: surprise, irritation and anger. Taking the phone, the professor dials the first number. - Hello, and Vasya can? - And here such does not live. "There," said the professor, smiling, "this is a slight surprise. Then dials the number again. - Hello, and Vasya did not fit? - Yes there are no such. Rubbing her hands, the professor conspiratorially winks at the audience and dials the third time. - So Vasya is not? - Yes you have gone! "Well, I hope the example is clear." From the first desk a student gets up. "Forgive me, Professor, but you have forgotten the fourth stage." - This is what? - Stage ohuyvaniya. Approaching the chair, the student dials a number. - Good afternoon. This is Vasya. Nobody called me?

Well, listen. The case was on December 31. It's ten o'clock in the evening. We are going to celebrate the New Year. We carry three decent bags - it is clear with what. Well, they themselves, of course, are no longer sober. And now, only 5 minutes remain, we are already looking forward to the fun, and suddenly ... At ten meters, the police car brakes gently. From there the sergeant climbs and heads straight for us. Thoughts, naturally, are the darkest - at best money shakes, at worst - the New Year in the monkey. But how will you meet ... Sergeant comes up: - Put the bags! - dutifully put bags on the snow. To my friend: - Hands stretch, - he stretches out his hands. - A handful! - he puts his hands in a surprised handful. The sergeant climbs into his pocket and pours a handful of chocolates into his hands. Then he turns and goes to the car. From the car turns around and says: - Well, you are waiting for some miracle in the New Year!

Well, listen. The schoolgirl is going to the prom. She approaches her mother: - Mom, and if I kiss with the boy on the lips, then I can get pregnant? Well, listen. A competent insurance salesman: The newly arrived soldiers were driven into the room, the Insurer stepped onto the stage, smiled and said: "Guys, I know that you do not want to listen to all kinds of stuff for a long time, so I'll explain to you about the insurances in one minute. The meaning of insurance is simple: if you do not have it, you were sent into battle and you were killed - the government will send your family a letter of condolences. If you have it, you were sent into battle and you were killed - the government will send your family $ 20,000. Then he paused, looked around the room with his eyes and asked: "What do you think, guys, who will be the first to be sent into battle in this situation?"

Well, listen. One person has committed a crime. He was caught and brought to court to the king. The death penalty was reserved for his deed, but the king offered to choose his own destiny: either to be hanged, or to get behind a big, black, terrible steel door. The criminal thought and chose the gallows. When a loop was thrown around his neck, he suddenly said: "I was curious: what is there, behind that door?" The king laughed: "Yes, you see, it's a funny thing." I all offer this choice, and everyone chooses the gallows. "What's behind the door?" - asked the criminal. "I still will not tell anyone," he added, pointing to the noose. After a pause, the king replied: "There's freedom." But people are so afraid of the unknown that they prefer a rope!
